rationale: DC contact charging only starts above the air-gap threshold (~500-650 V); reaching a -600 V drum surface needs |bias| ~ |V_0| + V_th. Rev A -720 V nominal cannot charge the drum it assumes.
AC (>= 2 x V_th peak-to-peak) superposed on DC converges the surface to the DC value and cancels threshold drift; costs an AC HV stage.
Verified by the Rev C voltage ladder: with a real -600 V surface this bias holds >= 150 V development contrast and >= 100 V fog margin across the OPC sensitivity band and the V_th uncertainty band.
Unchanged in Rev C; transfer optimization is owned by hypothesis H5 (paper impedance sniff) once a paper path exists.
